guidebook.title: New:Sim-U-Kraft Pioneer Handbook
guidebook.subtitle: City Building from Beginner to Master
guidebook.open_hint: Left-click or right-click to open the page
guidebook.cover.title: New:Sim-U-Kraft Pioneer Handbook
guidebook.cover.subtitle: City Building from Beginner to Master
guidebook.item.name: New:Sim-U-Kraft Pioneer Handbook
guidebook.directory.title: Contents
guidebook.directory.description: Welcome to New:Sim-U-Kraft! This handbook will help you get started with city building quickly.
guidebook.directory.prologue.name: Prologue
guidebook.directory.prologue.desc: The story of becoming a city pioneer
guidebook.directory.city.name: City System
guidebook.directory.city.desc: City core, build box, and the basics of city development
guidebook.directory.commerce.name: Commerce System
guidebook.directory.commerce.desc: Commercial control box, shop management and finances
guidebook.directory.logistics.name: Logistics System
guidebook.directory.logistics.desc: Logistics server, client and network connections
guidebook.chapter.prologue.title: Prologue
guidebook.chapter.prologue.page1.left_1: $player_name, perhaps you are still tired of the days of wandering,
guidebook.chapter.prologue.page1.left_2: longing for some peaceful days, so you became a city pioneer——
guidebook.chapter.prologue.page1.right_1: Hey kid, being a mayor isn't easy, you may not get the peaceful life you want, but it's certainly better than wandering around. Take this book with you, you'll surely find it useful!
guidebook.directory.items.name: Item Encyclopedia
guidebook.directory.items.desc: Detailed introduction of the mod's core items
guidebook.chapter.items.page1.left_desc: These items are the heart of the mod. Understanding their functions is the first step to building a city.
guidebook.chapter.items.city_core: City Core: An essential item for creating a city. Place it and right-click to create your city~
guidebook.chapter.items.build_box: Build Box: Hire architects to construct different categories of buildings, or hire planners to plan areas!
guidebook.chapter.items.residential_box: Residential Control Box: Appears in residential buildings and stores the building's information.
guidebook.chapter.items.commercial_box: Commercial Control Box: Appears in commercial buildings. You can hire NPC shopkeepers here.
guidebook.chapter.items.industrial_box: Industrial Control Box: Appears in industrial buildings. You can hire workers for production here.
guidebook.chapter.items.logistics_server: Logistics Server: Placed in your city's warehouse, it is the heart of the logistics network.
guidebook.chapter.items.logistics_client: Logistics Client: Place it where the city needs items, connecting containers to the logistics network.
guidebook.chapter.items.page2.left_desc: These auxiliary items make managing the city much more convenient.
guidebook.chapter.items.farmland_box: Farmland Box: The core of automated agricultural production. Hire farmers here to start farming.
guidebook.chapter.items.hint: Tip: Click an item icon to view its crafting recipe (if JEI is installed).
guidebook.chapter.items.page1_right_el0: Sim-U-Kraft adds the following special foods, fitting the daily life of citizens~
guidebook.chapter.items.page2_right_el1: Guidebook: This very book you're reading. Open it any time for help.
start.left_title: Getting Started: Create a New City
start.intro: The origin of every beginning, a ray of bright morning sun — you start a new adventure, but this time, you become the Mayor.
guidebook.chapter.start.page0_left_el2: This is a City Core. You can create your city using this block.
guidebook.chapter.start.page0_left_el3: The city name must not duplicate an existing one. It must be at least 2 characters and contain no special symbols.
guidebook.chapter.start.page0_right_el0: If the "World Population" in the upper right changes and a citizen joins your city, your city has been successfully created.
guidebook.chapter.start.page0_right_el1: If you can't find your citizen, search carefully. If still not found, creation may have failed.
guidebook.chapter.start.page0_right_el3: Once placed, the City Core cannot be moved. If absolutely necessary, go to City Core → Edit City → Delete City to delete and recreate it, but your funds and citizens will also be lost.
guidebook.chapter.start.page1_left_el0: You've created your city — well done! Now let's start building your first house with us.
guidebook.chapter.start.page1_left_el2: Tip: you can use JEI to look up crafting recipes~
guidebook.chapter.start.page0_left_el4: Hire an architect first to handle construction! Start with your first citizen and let them build their own home.
guidebook.chapter.start.page1_left_el4: Choose a building that suits you
guidebook.chapter.start.page1_right_el0: Apart from the "Studio Unit", most other buildings require some construction materials. We recommend building this one on your first day.
guidebook.tooltip.start: Jump to the Getting Started chapter
guidebook.chapter.start.page2_left_el0: For each block placed, the NPC gains 1 XP. Leveling up improves work efficiency.
guidebook.chapter.items.page3_left_el2: Cheese chunks are the product of the cheese factory. They restore 2 hunger points and can be used at "KenDaJi" to make a cheeseburger~
guidebook.chapter.items.page3_right_el1: Cheeseburger: made from bread + beef + cheese chunk at "KenDaJi". Restores 12 hunger points.
guidebook.chapter.items.page3_right_el3: Beef Burger: made from bread + beef at "KenDaJi". Restores 8 hunger points.
guidebook.chapter.items.page4_left_el1: French fries are made from potatoes at "KenDaJi". Restores 4 hunger points.
guidebook.chapter.start.page2_left_el1: After choosing a building, double-click to open the details, then click "Preview" to enter preview mode.
guidebook.chapter.start.page1_right_el2: Pick a residential building first! Note: building anything consumes money, materials, and time.
guidebook.chapter.start.page2_left_el2: Use WSAD to move the camera, arrow keys ↑↓←→ to move the preview, +/- to lower or raise its height, and Enter to start construction.
guidebook.chapter.start.page2_left_el4: This mod adds a building preview feature, letting you place a building precisely without having to move the build box.
guidebook.chapter.start.page2_right_el1: Your first building has begun construction! While waiting, you can gather more materials in preparation for what comes next.
guidebook.chapter.start.page2_right_el2: Each block placed grants the architect 1 XP. As experience grows, the NPC levels up and works more efficiently.
guidebook.chapter.start.page3_left_el0: Now that your first citizen has a home, it's time to develop your city, pioneer!
guidebook.chapter.start.page3_left_el1: If there are vacant residences in the city, a new citizen will join your city at noon (6000 ticks) of each day.
guidebook.chapter.start.page3_left_el2: Since the "Studio Unit" rent is low, you'll likely still need to spend starter funds on day two. Time to build something else!
guidebook.chapter.start.page3_right_el1: Every morning, citizens pay rent equal to 30% of the building's construction price.
guidebook.chapter.start.page3_right_el2: Therefore, only building cheap homes like the "Studio Unit" spares you material gathering, but yields very little rent.
guidebook.chapter.start.page3_right_el3: Stock up materials now and build something pricier (say, $5–7)!
guidebook.chapter.start.page4_left_el1: After developing your city this way, you may notice a problem: your citizens are starving!
guidebook.chapter.start.page4_left_el2: That's right — citizens have hunger too. When hungry, their work efficiency drops, and they may even die.
guidebook.chapter.start.page4_right_el0: The simplest way is to feed citizens manually. From enchanted golden apples to rotten flesh — toss any food at a citizen to restore their hunger.
guidebook.chapter.start.page4_right_el1: Of course, manually tossing food gets exhausting with many citizens. As mayor, we have a better way.
guidebook.chapter.start.page4_right_el2: This is where commercial buildings come into play.
guidebook.chapter.start.page5_left_el1: Commercial buildings are diverse — some sell food to citizens, some sell resources to players, and some buy items from players, providing them with currency.
guidebook.chapter.start.page5_left_el2: To feed citizens, we can build food shops — like meat stalls, butcher shops, fruit shops, and bakeries.
guidebook.chapter.start.page5_right_el0: For example, we start with the "Meat Stall", a simple building that sells chicken to citizens. You need to provide raw chicken; hire a butcher who will cook it and sell the cooked chicken to hungry citizens!
guidebook.chapter.start.page5_right_el2: and so do other shops that sell to citizens~
guidebook.chapter.directory.page0_right_: Teach you to build a city from scratch
guidebook.chapter.start.page6_left_el1: Tired of gathering building materials and food by hand? You need industrial buildings!
guidebook.chapter.start.page6_left_el2: After constructing an industrial building, right-click its control box to open the interface.
guidebook.chapter.start.page6_left_el3: Once you hire a worker, production begins according to the chosen recipe. Production speed depends on NPC level.
guidebook.chapter.start.page6_right_el0: Another great place to obtain building materials — the Building Material Merchant.
guidebook.chapter.start.page6_right_el1: The Building Material Merchant is a commercial building. Once a shopkeeper is hired, it sells various building materials to players.
guidebook.chapter.start.page7_left_el0: Another great place to obtain building materials — the Building Material Merchant.
guidebook.chapter.start.page7_left_el1: The Building Material Merchant is a commercial building. Once a shopkeeper is hired, it sells various building materials to players.
guidebook.chapter.start.page7_right_el0: Likewise, the Lumberjack's Cabin can provide various kinds of logs and stripped logs.
guidebook.chapter.start.page7_right_el1: Note, however, that these commercial buildings have stock limits — you can't buy infinitely.
guidebook.chapter.start.page7_right_el2: When stock runs out, wait for the shopkeeper to restock the next day. You can also build multiple shops to expand stock.
guidebook.chapter.start.page8_left_el1: To save the labor of moving items between commercial and industrial buildings, you can set up a logistics system.
guidebook.chapter.start.page8_left_el2: Start by building a warehouse! Design one stocked with containers and place a "Logistics Server" inside.
guidebook.chapter.start.page8_right_el1: If you want to start quickly, choose a warehouse from the "Other" category in the Build Box and let citizens build it for you~
guidebook.chapter.start.page9_left_el0: After placing the logistics server, hire a warehouse manager and bind a container.
guidebook.chapter.start.page9_left_el1: Then, place a logistics client near where items need to be transferred and bind a container to it as well.
guidebook.chapter.start.page9_left_el2: Almost every commercial and industrial building comes with a built-in logistics client. You can bind containers directly through it.
guidebook.chapter.start.page9_right_el0: Logistics is great, but it's not free.
guidebook.chapter.start.page9_right_el1: Creating a warehouse costs $2 per container. For routes longer than 256 blocks, each stack of items transferred costs $0.02; for every 64 blocks beyond 256, an additional $0.01 per stack.
guidebook.chapter.start.page9_right_el2: Smart, economical city layouts reduce logistics costs!
guidebook.chapter.start.page10_left_el3: The mod offers many buildings — feel free to construct them one by one and enjoy the joy of full collection!
guidebook.chapter.start.page10_left_el1: Now, you have plenty of rent income, with commercial and industrial facilities providing food and materials. Congratulations on building your very own metropolis from the wilderness!
guidebook.chapter.start.page10_right_el1: As the city grows, old buildings (like "Studio Unit") may look out of place. Hire a planner to demolish them, then manually remove the building's control box. Residents will automatically move to new buildings.
guidebook.chapter.start.page10_right_el3: Many building expansion packs are available in the community to further enrich your city!
guidebook.chapter.start.page11_left_el0: After this tutorial, you should now understand the mod's basic principles and mechanics.
guidebook.chapter.start.page11_left_el1: There's still much to explore, and we plan to keep innovating with more interesting features in the future!
guidebook.chapter.start.page11_left_el2: In short — thank you, mayors, for downloading this mod! If you run into issues, please report them to our official group!
guidebook.chapter.items.page2_left_el4:
Manifest: sneak + right-click on a build box
to get the material list for the current task.
